summary: All three prior objections are substantially resolved; OBJ-1 (AC4 rewritten as positive assertion) and OBJ-2 (M3/AC5 added for docstring fix) are resolved with one non-blocking editorial note on a contradictory Technical Hints bullet; OBJ-3 (evidence_type) remains deferred as non-blocking.

objections.title: AC4 rewritten as positive assertion — RESOLVED
objections.prior_state: AC4 was a no-op negative assertion ('does NOT contain X') where X was already absent from the file, making it trivially pass on the unmodified hook.
objections.revised_state: AC4 now reads: 'GIVEN _block_default_deny_commit() after the change, WHEN read, THEN the message CONTAINS text indicating that the main agent may use /allow with a matching pattern (e.g. the string "Main agent may bypass with /allow" is present). AND the test fails on the unmodified file (current message at lines 433-446 contains no reference to /allow — confirmed by Read).'
objections.resolution_assessment: RESOLVED. The positive assertion ('CONTAINS ... /allow') is verifiable and fails on the unmodified file (confirmed: lines 433-446 contain no '/allow' reference). The 'AND the test fails on the unmodified file' clause prevents a no-op pass. M2 in Must Have section is aligned: it explicitly acknowledges the phrase 'It can't be bypassed from inside the session' does not exist and prescribes additive-only change. Technical Hints item 2 is consistent.
evidence: ticket-20260517-222440.md AC4: 'the message CONTAINS text indicating that the main agent may use /allow with a matching pattern'
ticket-20260517-222440.md AC4 evidence note: 'AND the test fails on the unmodified file (current message at lines 433-446 contains no reference to /allow — confirmed by Read)'
M2 in Must Have section: 'Add one line: Main agent may bypass with /allow <pattern> before the git commit command.'
Technical Hints item 2: 'do NOT remove any existing text; the phrase It can't be bypassed from inside the session does not exist in the file and there is nothing to remove'

objections.title: _check_git_allowlist() docstring gap — RESOLVED (with minor editorial note on Technical Hints wording)
objections.prior_state: BA did not address the docstring 'non-commit/' scope discrepancy, and the 'Do not modify: _check_git_allowlist() itself' prohibition actively blocked the docstring fix.
objections.revised_state: M3 added as a Must Have requirement prescribing the one-token docstring edit. AC5 added as a dedicated acceptance criterion. Technical Hints item 3 explicitly prescribes the edit at line 142. The 'Do not modify' final bullet is retained verbatim but lists _check_git_allowlist() alongside behavioral functions (_has_do_consent, _evaluate_push, _find_grant, _find_grant_any).
objections.resolution_assessment: SUBSTANTIALLY RESOLVED. M3 is a normative requirement; AC5 is a normative acceptance criterion. Technical Hints item 3 is an explicit prescription. A dev following the spec can execute this correctly. The residual issue is editorial: the final Technical Hints bullet 'Do not modify: _check_git_allowlist() itself' is a literal contradiction of item 3, but (a) item 3 is more specific and takes precedence as a matter of spec interpretation, (b) all other entries in the 'Do not modify' list are behavioral functions, suggesting the intent is 'logic/behavior only', (c) the normative requirements (M3, AC5) are unambiguous. This is a non-blocking wording defect that BA should fix.

minor_editorial_note.finding: Final bullet reads 'Do not modify: _check_git_allowlist() itself' which literally contradicts Technical Hints item 3 prescribing the docstring edit at line 142. The bullet was intended to protect behavioral logic, not docstrings.
minor_editorial_note.required_fix: Change 'Do not modify: _check_git_allowlist() itself' to 'Do not modify: _check_git_allowlist() logic or behavior (docstring update per item 3 above is permitted)'.
minor_editorial_note.rationale: Dev can resolve the contradiction by applying the more specific instruction (item 3) over the general prohibition. M3 and AC5 are normative and unambiguous. Codex adversarial review confirmed this is reconcilable.

evidence: ticket-20260517-222440.md M3: 'Remove it so the docstring reads Check /allow grant for non-push git operations. Read-only. (one-token edit at line 142 only; no behavioral change)'
ticket-20260517-222440.md AC5: 'THEN the docstring does NOT contain "non-commit/" and reads "Check /allow grant for non-push git operations. Read-only." AND no other line of the function is modified'

ticket-20260517-222440.md Technical Hints item 3: 'In _check_git_allowlist() at line 142: change the docstring from ... to ... (remove the 11-character token "non-commit/" from the first line of the docstring only)'
ticket-20260517-222440.md Technical Hints final bullet: 'Do not modify: _check_git_allowlist() itself' — literal contradiction of item 3; editorial defect, non-blocking

objections.title: evidence_type='inferred' conflicts with live observation — UNRESOLVED BUT NON-BLOCKING (deferred)
objections.prior_state: context-20260517-222440.json evidence_type='inferred' conflicts with the live evidence observation in the same JSON.
objections.revised_state: evidence_type field remains 'inferred' in the context JSON. The live evidence observation is still present. Each observation now explicitly carries '(inferred from Read)' qualifier, clarifying scope, but the field value itself is not updated.
objections.resolution_assessment: NOT RESOLVED. The minor inconsistency remains. However, this does not affect M1 correctness, scope accuracy, affected file accuracy, or any of the normative requirements. Accepted for deferral — no implementation risk.

evidence_quality.rationale: M1 evidence is strong and unchanged. M2 positive evidence is correct (additive-only change, no phantom text to remove — confirmed by Read+grep). M3 evidence is correct (docstring confirmed at line 142, one-token fix). No new false factual claims introduced in revision.

scope_alignment.rationale: BA correctly captures R1. M3 is a correct scope addition — it addresses a correctness defect in the docstring that would be created by M1. No scope inflation. Won't Have items unchanged and still appropriate.

investigation_completeness.rationale: BA explicitly confirmed by Read+grep that 'It can't be bypassed from inside the session' does not exist in the hook (zero matches across hooks/). This investigation was the gap in iteration 1. The docstring at line 142 is confirmed by Read. M2 and M3 are grounded in observed file content.

affected_file_accuracy.rationale: All three change targets (line 449+ in _evaluate_commit, lines 433-446 in _block_default_deny_commit, line 142 in _check_git_allowlist) are accurately cited. The Technical Hints item numbering and line references are consistent with the file read in the BA session.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.rationale: AC4 is now a positive assertion with a 'test fails on unmodified file' guard. AC5 uses a combined positive+negative formulation (does NOT contain 'non-commit/' AND reads the correct text) — both halves are verifiable against the current file state. No drift between AC text and what grep/Read would actually verify.

passing_elements: M1 unchanged and correct — core fix (_check_git_allowlist call in _evaluate_commit) is well-specified
M2 now correctly prescribes additive change only with no phantom text removal
M3 added as Must Have — docstring correctness fix is normative
AC4 is a positive assertion that fails on unmodified file — verifiable
AC5 added — docstring fix is verifiable
Technical Hints items 1-3 are all actionable and unambiguous
Won't Have items (W1-W4) unchanged and appropriate
All line number references consistent between ticket, context JSON, and component_chain
Grant double-consumption analysis unchanged and correct
Subagent exclusion analysis unchanged and correct
Insertion order constraint (BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE first) explicit in both M1 and Technical Hints

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed: (1) The Technical Hints contradiction is real but not a hard blocker — M3/AC5 clearly require only a docstring edit and the final bullet was intended to forbid logic changes. PROPOSED_FIX: revise final bullet to 'Do not modify _check_git_allowlist() logic or behavior; only update its docstring as specified.' (2) Verdict of 'fail' may be too strict for a reconcilable wording conflict in non-normative Technical Hints guidance. PROPOSED_FIX: change verdict to 'pass with required editorial cleanup'. (3) OBJ-3 classification should be explicit about non-blocking status. PROPOSED_FIX: reword as 'UNRESOLVED BUT NON-BLOCKING'. (4) OBSERVATION_ONLY: specs should not rely on developer inference even when contradiction is reconcilable.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Verdict changed from 'fail' to 'pass' per Codex finding 2: the contradiction is in non-normative Technical Hints guidance, not in normative requirements (M3) or acceptance criteria (AC5). The minor editorial note on Technical Hints wording is flagged but marked non-blocking. OBJ-3 reclassified as 'UNRESOLVED BUT NON-BLOCKING' per Codex finding 3. BA editorial action requested (non-blocking).

ba_editorial_action_requested.action: Fix Technical Hints final bullet: change 'Do not modify: _check_git_allowlist() itself' to 'Do not modify: _check_git_allowlist() logic or behavior (docstring update per item 3 above is permitted)'. This is a one-line editorial fix; dev can proceed without waiting for it given M3 and AC5 are unambiguous.
